topic.what: Systematic review and meta-analysis of Yukgunja-tang (Korean name for Liu Jun Zi Tang / Rikkunshito) for the treatment of chronic atrophic gastritis, pooling evidence from Korean, Japanese, and Chinese clinical trials.
topic.how: Systematic literature search across PubMed, Cochrane, CNKI, KISS, and J-STAGE databases; meta-analysis using random-effects models; outcomes including symptom scores, endoscopic mucosal improvement, and inflammatory marker changes; GRADE evidence grading.
topic.why_matters: Provides highest-tier synthesized evidence for Liu Jun Zi Tang / Rikkunshito / Yukgunja-tang across the pan-East-Asian tradition, establishing that the same formula used in China, Japan, and Korea converges on consistent clinical benefit for atrophic gastritis and validating cross-cultural generalizability of the herbal evidence base.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Pooled standardized mean difference in validated dyspepsia/atrophic gastritis symptom scores
characteristics.value: Statistically significant pooled improvement across included trials
characteristics.context: Pooled across Korean, Japanese, and Chinese RCTs using random-effects model

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate of endoscopically confirmed atrophic gastritis mucosal improvement or regression
characteristics.value: Significant improvement in responder rate vs. control
characteristics.vs_baseline: Higher mucosal responder rate vs. conventional treatment or placebo
characteristics.context: Endoscopic outcomes less consistently reported across trials; subgroup analysis

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree of heterogeneity in treatment effects across Chinese, Japanese, and Korean trial cohorts
characteristics.value: Low-to-moderate heterogeneity (I² consistent with acceptable pooling)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Consistent effect direction across all three national traditions
characteristics.context: Supports pan-East-Asian generalizability of Liu Jun Zi Tang formula evidence

characteristics.what_it_measures: Change in serum or mucosal inflammatory markers (e.g., IL-6, TNF-alpha, PGE2) associated with atrophic gastritis
characteristics.value: Significant reduction in inflammatory markers in treatment group
characteristics.vs_baseline: Reduced vs. control at trial endpoint
characteristics.context: Mechanistically consistent with anti-inflammatory properties of formula components

good_for: Clinicians treating chronic atrophic gastritis in East Asian populations
Researchers synthesizing pan-East-Asian traditional medicine evidence
Guideline developers in Korea, Japan, and China updating atrophic gastritis recommendations
Drug developers seeking validated natural-product leads for atrophic gastritis
not_for: Patients with autoimmune atrophic gastritis (distinct pathophysiology from H. pylori-associated)
Western populations where formula standardization and regulatory status differ
requires: Access to standardized Liu Jun Zi Tang / Yukgunja-tang / Rikkunshito preparations
Confirmed atrophic gastritis diagnosis (endoscopic or histological)
Understanding of cross-national naming conventions for the same formula

novelty: First meta-analysis explicitly pooling Korean, Japanese, and Chinese trial data for the same pan-East-Asian formula under its Korean designation (Yukgunja-tang)
Establishes highest-tier evidence (meta-analysis) for Liu Jun Zi Tang in chronic atrophic gastritis specifically, beyond the functional dyspepsia evidence base
Demonstrates cross-cultural formula identity convergence, strengthening the argument for unified international evidence standards for shared East Asian herbal formulas
open_problems: Publication bias risk from predominance of Asian-language trials in the evidence base
Heterogeneity in formula preparation standards across Korean, Japanese, and Chinese manufacturers not fully resolved
Optimal treatment duration for atrophic gastritis (vs. functional dyspepsia) not established
Whether mucosal improvement translates to reduced gastric cancer risk requires long-term prospective study
Interaction with H. pylori eradication therapy not systematically evaluated in included trials
